source_intake_record.raw_question_text: In a simple model for a radioactive nucleus, an alpha particle } \( m = 6.64 \times 10^{-27} \text{ kg} \) \text{ is trapped by a square barrier that has width } \( 2.0 \text{ fm} \) \text{ and height } \( 30.0 \text{ MeV} \) What is the tunneling probability when the alpha particle encounters the barrier if its kinetic energy is \( 1.0 \text{ MeV} \) below the top of the barrier as shown in figure?

image_dependency: question explicitly says 'as shown in the figure'
image_manifest shows 1 existing image with readable dimensions 360x344
multi_step: solving requires applying a tunneling/barrier-penetration formula rather than direct lookup
the problem combines multiple quantities: mass, barrier width, barrier height, and energy relative to the barrier top
unit handling is needed across fm, MeV, and SI quantities
multiple-choice selection adds a final comparison step against 4 options
verifiability: choices provide 4 discrete candidate values for easy checking
registry_passed=true with no listed asset issues
the final answer format is a single scalar ('0.090'), which is straightforward to verify

decision_reasons: 资产完整：题干、答案和图片均存在，且 registry_passed=true、issues 为空
答案为单一数值且可验证性强，便于后续核验
虽有 figure_role_may_be_supportive_rather_than_strictly_essential 风险标记，但不足以构成核心语义缺失或明显质量风险
